title: Progress Test
objectives: Assess vocabulary knowledge from Unit 5 (celebrations, Ramadan, national events)
Test reading comprehension with a new passage
Evaluate grammar: present simple negative and prepositions of time
Assess guided writing about Eid Al-Fitr

exercises.problem: We visit our ............ on Fridays. (a. fireworks, b. grandparents, c. months)
In Hajj, we stay on ............ Arafat. (a. mount, b. birthday, c. holiday)
I usually ............ with my family on Kuwait's National Day. (a. forget, b. invite, c. march)
You are very ............ to have so many gifts. (a. lucky, b. tired, c. fizzy)
Read the passage about Abrar's first day of school. 1. The best title could be: (a. A Special Day, b. A Smiling Teacher, c. Playing Games)
The underlined word 'They' in line 7 refers to: (a. games, b. friends, c. teachers)
Abrar feels ............ to have so many friends at school. (a. small, b. lucky, c. new)
What does Abrar prepare for the first day of school?
exercises.answer: She puts on her new uniform and shoes, then prepares her backpack with pencils, notebooks, and a small snack.

exercises.problem: I don't ............ water before iftar. (a. drink, b. drank, c. drinking)
She doesn't ............ video games in Ramadan. (a. played, b. play, c. plays)
Muslims stay on Mount Arafat ............ the 9th day of Dhu Al-Hijjah. (a. at, b. in, c. on)
Write a paragraph about 'Eid Al-Fitr' with pictures and guide words: new-clothes, pray-mosque, visit-grandparents
exercises.answer: On Eid Al-Fitr, we wear new clothes. We pray in the mosque. We visit our grandparents. Eid Al-Fitr is a happy day.

exercises.problem: Complete: 1. We __________ every Ramadan. 2. They pray Al-Taraweeh __________ in the mosque. 3. __________ Arafat is in Makkah.

teaching_notes: This progress test assesses all four skills from Unit 5. The reading section uses a new passage (Abrar's first day of school) to test comprehension strategies learned in the unit. Grammar tests present simple negative (don't/doesn't + base verb) and prepositions of time (in/on/at). Writing requires a guided paragraph about Eid Al-Fitr.
